skill.name: share
skill.description: Create and update static p11 documents, then publish them as shareable, commentable review links with the p11 CLI.
skill.summary: Author polished p11 documents and publish shareable review links.
skill.author: rarexlabs

security_audit.summary: The static weak-cryptography findings are false positives caused by ordinary words in descriptions and component names. The Ruby backtick findings are mostly Markdown code spans, but the skill does instruct assistants to install and run an external npm CLI and publish documents through p11. No evidence found of prompt injection, obfuscation, credential theft, or malicious intent; publication should require clear user consent and careful handling of private edit URLs.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Installation and Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs assistants to install or invoke the p11 CLI, including a global npm install when the command is missing. This is legitimate for the workflow, but it executes third-party package code and changes the host environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly instructs installation and execution of an external CLI. The commands are purposeful and not obfuscated, so the risk is elevated but appears workflow-related.

medium_findings.title: Document Publication and Bearer Edit URL Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill publishes authored documents with p11 and instructs the assistant to output read and edit URLs. The edit URL is explicitly described as a bearer credential, so accidental disclosure could allow unauthorized document changes or deletion.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The publication commands and private edit URL handling are explicit in the instructions. There is no evidence of malicious exfiltration, but the workflow can expose sensitive document content or control links.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography locations contain ordinary descriptive text or component names, not cryptographic algorithms or security-sensitive hashing. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic use in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines contain user-facing description or component documentation, not code that performs cryptography. The static matches appear to be substring collisions.

low_findings.title: Most Backtick Execution Alerts Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: Many Ruby or shell backtick alerts point to Markdown code spans, TSX examples, and component lists. These references document p11 usage and component restrictions rather than executing Ruby backtick commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown prose or fenced examples, and no Ruby code is present. Separate CLI instructions remain captured as medium-risk external command findings.

dangerous_patterns.title: Global Latest Package Install
dangerous_patterns.description: The instruction to install the latest global p11 CLI creates supply-chain and host-environment risk if used without user approval or package verification.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is directly documented and would install executable npm package code globally. The intent is functional, not malicious.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Publishing Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The p11 share command publishes a document and returns links, which can expose sensitive content if the user did not intend external sharing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The sharing command and API override are explicit. The risk depends on user intent and document sensitivity.

content.value_statement: Teams often need polished documents that reviewers can open and comment on quickly. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create static p11 documents and publish review links.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation and editing of static React TSX documents for p11.
Uses document-safe exports from the p11 component library.
Runs p11 CLI help, documentation, example, share, and update commands.
Publishes new p11 documents with shareable read links.
Updates existing p11 documents when an edit URL is provided.
Reminds assistants to protect private edit URLs as bearer credentials.
limitations: Requires the p11 CLI or temporary npx access to publish documents.
Does not support interactive app screens, forms, navigation, or controls.
Can expose document content externally if used on sensitive material.
Depends on current p11 CLI behavior and hosted p11 service availability.

use_cases.title: Publish a Proposal Draft
use_cases.description: Create a polished proposal document and share it with reviewers through a commentable p11 link.

use_cases.title: Share an Engineering Spec
use_cases.description: Turn a technical plan into a static p11 document that reviewers can read without running the project.

use_cases.title: Update a Review Memo
use_cases.description: Revise an existing p11 document using its private edit URL and publish the updated version.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Share Document
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new document link.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to turn my notes into a clear p11 document and share it for review. Ask before publishing if any content seems sensitive.

prompt_templates.title: Format a Proposal for Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a p11 proposal with a short summary, goals, scope, timeline, risks, and open questions. Then share it and provide the read and edit URLs.

prompt_templates.title: Update an Existing Document
prompt_templates.scenario: Revision using an existing edit link.
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the existing p11 document using this edit URL. Preserve the structure, apply the requested changes, and return the new read and edit URLs.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Detailed Review Packet
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced document with tables and sections.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a p11 review packet with sections, tables, quotes, and page breaks where useful. Validate component usage, publish it, and note any risks before sharing links.

output_examples.input: Create a commentable launch brief from these release notes.
output_examples.output: A polished p11 launch brief with sections for summary, schedule, owner list, risks, and reviewer questions, followed by read and edit links.

output_examples.input: Update my existing p11 proposal with the new budget and timeline.
output_examples.output: An updated p11 proposal that preserves the original structure, includes the revised budget and timeline, and returns refreshed sharing links.

output_examples.input: Convert this decision memo into a document reviewers can annotate.
output_examples.output: A static p11 decision memo with clear headings, readable tables, and a commentable review link for stakeholders.

best_practices: Confirm user intent before publishing documents that may contain private information.
Use only document-safe p11 components and named imports from the component library.
Store edit URLs carefully because they can control future updates or deletion.
anti_patterns: Do not publish secrets, credentials, customer data, or confidential plans without approval.
Do not build interactive controls, forms, navigation, tabs, or app-shell UI in p11 documents.
Do not install or upgrade the p11 CLI when the existing command already works.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates or updates static p11 documents and publishes links for review and comments.

faq.question: Does it need the p11 CLI?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill relies on the p11 CLI for documentation, validation examples, publishing, and updates.

faq.question: Can it create interactive web apps?
faq.answer: No. It is limited to static document content and document-safe p11 components.

faq.question: What should I do with the edit URL?
faq.answer: Treat the edit URL as private. Anyone with it may be able to update or delete the document.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential documents?
faq.answer: Use caution. Publishing can expose document content externally, so confirm approval before sharing sensitive material.

faq.question: Can it update an existing p11 link?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the existing edit URL and the requested changes so the assistant can publish a new version.
